badge_full_details: Substance: mostly or entirely AI-generated | Form: AI-generated
badge_full_footer: Content produced by AI with limited human intervention
bloc_signature:
Declaration: [TCP/UP: AIC] (AI Content)
> Substance: mostly or entirely AI-generated | Form: AI-generated
> Content produced by AI with limited human intervention
sidebar_download_call: Need a Black & White variant or specific format?
sidebar_disclaimer: TCP/UP is a declarative protocol. The displayed label corresponds to the author's self-declaration about their creation process
philosophy_quote: "100% synthetic. The human defined the need, the machine executed the creation."
definition_text: This content was generated mostly or entirely by artificial intelligence. Human intervention is limited primarily to instruction, selection, assembly, or distribution.
usecase_text: Illustration, rapid prototyping, mass translation, data visualization, automatic summaries, code generation, etc.
example2_text: Translate a 200-page technical manual for immediate internal use (without proofreading).
example3_text: Ask AI to generate a complete Bash script to monitor log partition occupancy and send an alert.
example4_text: Ask a tool to summarize a 50-page report into 3 paragraphs without human intervention.
edgecase1_title: "I selected the best of three AI-generated texts. Is it AIC?"
edgecase1_text: Yes. Your intervention is limited to selection, not co-creation of substance or form. The content remains entirely generated.
edgecase2_title: "I changed three words in an AI-generated text. Can I move to ACE?"
edgecase2_text: No. Cosmetic modification is not substantial co-construction. To claim ACE, your intervention must have significantly influenced the substance or structure.
edgecase3_title: "I gave a very detailed prompt (500 words) describing exactly what I wanted. AIC or ACE?"
edgecase3_text: If your prompt already contains the entire intellectual architecture (thesis, arguments, structure), you can consider HCE. If AI nonetheless introduced angles or wording that influence the final meaning, ACE is safer. A simple prompt, even a long one, that says 'write me an article about X' falls under AIC.
edgecases_more_info: More details on critical cases and arbitration here
prompt1_text: "Generate an image of a modern office in the rain, cinematic style, 16:9 format"
prompt2_text: "Write a Bash script that checks if the /var/log partition exceeds 90% usage and sends an alert email to the admin if it does"
prompt3_text: "Summarize this 50-page annual report into one 5-line paragraph, focusing only on key figures."
prompt4_text: "Generate a poem in the style of Baudelaire on the theme of the sea and melancholy."
